skill.name: create-new-skills
skill.description: Creates Agent Skills for Claude Code and Codex using documented structure, progressive disclosure, examples, and testing guidance.
skill.summary: Guides users through creating, testing, and sharing reusable Agent Skills.
skill.author: EGAdams

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged shell commands, URLs, hidden .claude paths, weak cryptography keywords, and C2-like terms. AI review found no executable bundled code, no prompt injection attempt, and no evidence of data exfiltration; most high-risk alerts are documentation false positives. Residual risk is medium because the skill guides agents through local filesystem, git, and deletion examples that require user oversight.

medium_findings.title: Local Command Guidance Requires User Oversight
medium_findings.description: The skill teaches agents to create directories, change permissions, inspect files, and run git commands. This matches the skill purpose, but it can modify local projects if commands are executed without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and operational, but they are documentation examples for a skill-authoring workflow. No evidence shows hidden execution or malicious command construction.

medium_findings.title: Destructive Removal Example Is Constrained But Sensitive
medium_findings.description: The documentation includes recursive removal examples for personal and project skill directories. The targets are constrained to skill folders, but agents should validate paths before running them.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static recursive-delete finding is real, but context shows a documented skill removal workflow. Risk remains if the path is changed or copied without checking.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography And C2 Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static alerts for weak cryptography and C2 keywords occur in natural-language documentation about Claude, skills, metadata, and examples. No cryptographic API use or command-and-control behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed context is prose and examples rather than runnable crypto or network-control code. The repeated high-severity static hits do not match the file semantics.

low_findings.title: Hidden Directory Access Is Expected Skill Storage
low_findings.description: References to .claude and ~/.claude/skills describe standard personal and project skill locations. They are expected for this skill and do not show hidden persistence or unauthorized access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths are documented installation locations for Claude Code skills. I did not find evidence of stealthy home-directory scanning or persistence beyond normal skill storage.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation References
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs point to Anthropic documentation, help pages, image assets, and a cookbook repository. The skill does not include code that automatically fetches or exfiltrates data to those URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are cited as documentation or embedded markdown image references. No evidence found of network calls executed by the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Recursive Delete Command Appears In Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation contains rm -rf examples for removing skill directories. This is not malicious in context, but it is a dangerous pattern if copied with an incorrect path.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is present exactly as a removal example. The target path is scoped, so the risk depends on user review before execution.

content.user_title: Create Reusable Claude and Codex Skills
content.value_statement: Teams need repeatable ways to package domain knowledge for AI agents. This skill guides users through structured Agent Skill creation, testing, and sharing.
actual_capabilities: Explains the required SKILL.md structure and YAML frontmatter fields.
Guides users through defining skill purpose, triggers, and scope.
Shows how to organize optional scripts, templates, examples, and reference files.
Provides examples for code review, data analysis, documentation, and skill extension.
Includes testing and sharing steps for project and personal skill directories.
limitations: It is guidance-only and does not include executable helper scripts.
It assumes the user understands basic Markdown, YAML, and filesystem concepts.
It focuses on Claude Code style skills and may need adaptation for other platforms.
It does not validate generated skills automatically.

use_cases.title: Create Team Workflow Skills
use_cases.description: Package repeatable project practices into shared skills that team members can use through Claude Code or Codex.

use_cases.title: Document Domain Expertise
use_cases.description: Turn procedures, examples, and reference material into a structured skill that agents can load when needed.

use_cases.title: Extend Existing Skills
use_cases.description: Add new instructions, examples, or supporting files to an existing skill without losing focus.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user creating a first skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new Agent Skill for [task]. Ask me the required setup questions before writing the skill structure.

prompt_templates.title: Design Skill Scope
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs help defining triggers and boundaries
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me define the purpose, trigger phrases, required tools, and limitations for a skill about [domain].

prompt_templates.title: Build Multi-File Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate user needs scripts or reference files
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a multi-file skill for [workflow]. Include a focused SKILL.md plan and supporting file recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Review and Improve Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user wants a quality review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this existing skill for scope, trigger quality, progressive disclosure, security concerns, and missing examples.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for reviewing Python code.
output: A concise skill purpose and trigger description.
A planned SKILL.md structure with review steps.
Suggested examples for single-file and multi-file reviews.

output_examples.input: Build a data analysis skill with scripts.
output: A recommended folder layout for instructions, scripts, and templates.
Guidance for documenting dependencies and script usage.
Testing steps using sample CSV data.
A focused change plan for adding supporting reference material.
Suggested workflow updates for the existing instructions.
Test cases for common correction scenarios.

best_practices: Keep each skill focused on one clear capability or workflow.
Use specific descriptions that include both purpose and trigger phrases.
Split large or optional context into referenced supporting files.
anti_patterns: Do not combine unrelated workflows into one broad skill.
Do not use vague descriptions that make skill activation unpredictable.
Do not include destructive commands without clear target validation.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create Agent Skills with structured instructions, metadata, examples, and optional supporting files.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance is written for Claude Code style skills and references Claude Code file locations.

faq.question: Can it help Codex users?
faq.answer: Yes. Codex users can apply the same structure when creating reusable agent instructions and workflow assets.

faq.question: Does it generate scripts automatically?
faq.answer: It explains when scripts are useful and how to reference them, but it does not include built-in script generators.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a warning because it teaches filesystem and shell workflows that require user review.

faq.question: What should users review before running commands?
faq.answer: Users should verify paths, command targets, permissions, and any git operations before execution.
